title: eulophia coddii
intro: Eulophia coddii, also known as the Codd's orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It is native to South Africa, particularly in the Eastern Cape and KwaZulu-Natal provinces. Eulophia coddii is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever and treating respiratory infections, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for use.
